data.title: Earnings Snapshot : Citigroup tops Q1 estimates, trims FY2025 revenue forecast
Apple to exclusively use OLED panels from Samsung for 1st foldable iPhone - report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) has decided to exclusively use OLED panels from Samsung Display for its first foldable iPhone, which is set to launch in the latter half of next year, Business Korea reported.

This decision marks a vital moment in the industry as Samsung Display will be the sole supplier, excluding LG Display and Chinese BOE panels. The measure shows Samsung Electronics' (OTCPK:SSNLF) technological edge in minimizing screen creases, a key factor in foldable phone design, the report added.

The South Korean tech intends to start full-scale supply by the end of this year or early next year. The foldable iPhone is expected to have a premium OLED panel similar to the 7.8-inch iPad Mini tablet PC, with an external display expected to be a 5.5-inch OLED, according to the report.

The product could be priced at about $2,000, and it would compete directly with Samsung’s own Galaxy Z Fold, the report noted.

Apple and Samsung did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Apple's plan to rely solely on Samsung Display is important because of the iPhone maker's usual strategy of using several suppliers to drive down costs while maintaining high quality. Industry sources noted that Samsung’s six years of expertise in foldable OLED technology since launching the Galaxy Fold in 2019 has played a vital role in securing this exclusive contract, the report added.

“For Apple, which prioritizes quality over price when selecting component suppliers, Samsung was the only viable choice,” the report added, citing an industry insider.

The volume of supply for Apple’s foldable iPhone has not been determined yet. However, industry expectations indicate that it may be more than the initially projected annual level of 9 million units, potentially touching over 15 million units, the report noted.

This substantial demand could reignite growth in the foldable OLED market, which saw a small increase of 2.9% last year, according to Counterpoint Research.

This strategic collaboration is anticipated to impact Samsung’s market share in foldable OLEDs, which fell to nearly 40% last year due to competition from rivals such as Chinese company BOE. With deliveries starting soon, Samsung’s market share is forecasted to grow significantly to the 70% range next year, as per the report.

The U.S. tech company's entry into the foldable segment is expected to excite high-income North American customers and may be a catalyst for renewed interest in the entire market.

data.title: Air Products shares slip on downgrade at Bank of America
data.description:
Air Products and Chemicals’ (NYSE:APD) shares fell about 2% in premarket trading Tuesday after being downgraded by analysts at Bank of America to Underperform from a previous investment rating of Neutral. They warned that the company faces growing headwinds across its clean hydrogen portfolio and key industrial markets.

The downgrade reflects heightened uncertainty around the financial viability and timing of three major clean hydrogen projects in Saudi Arabia (Neom), Louisiana and Alberta. Analysts flagged a deteriorating outlook driven by global trade tensions under the Trump administration, weakening policy support for climate initiatives, and growing reluctance among potential customers to commit to long-term contracts at viable price points.

While new leadership at Air Products has cut underperforming initiatives, the remaining marquee projects now carry elevated risk, Bank of America wrote. Tariffs and weakened climate policy could inflate capital costs and delay timelines, particularly for Neom and Louisiana, the bank said.

The Neom project, once considered a cornerstone of Air Products’ (NYSE:APD) clean hydrogen ambitions, is particularly vulnerable. Its primary market, Europe, is showing signs of backing away from green hydrogen targets in favor of blue hydrogen — a development that could weaken demand. Meanwhile, the Louisiana facility may struggle with diminished domestic interest amid a shifting U.S. regulatory landscape, including challenges to the EPA’s Endangerment Finding.

In contrast, the Alberta project was seen as a relative bright spot. Despite a likely delay beyond its planned late-2025 startup, it benefits from existing pipeline infrastructure and ongoing carbon capture incentives from the Canadian government. If executed effectively, it could offer a path to recovery for Air Products (APD), the analysts said.

Bank of America also cited broader macroeconomic pressures affecting the company’s traditional markets — energy, chemicals, metals and manufacturing — which together account for over two-thirds of its business. As a result, the firm trimmed its fiscal 2025 earnings estimate to $12.65 a share, slightly less than Air Products’ (APD) guidance range of $12.70 to $13 a share.

Looking ahead, fiscal 2026 earnings are forecast at $13.82 a share, assuming trade negotiations stabilize. Still, the bank lowered its valuation multiple and long-term growth assumptions, cutting its price target to $282 from $330 a share.

data.title: Albertsons Companies' cautious FY25 EPS guidance weighs on shares
data.description:
Shares of Albertsons Companies (NYSE:ACI) are losing ground ahead of Tuesday’s opening bell as the grocery chain’s soft EPS guidance offset a better-than-expected profit in the fourth quarter.

With fiscal 2025 expected to be “an investment year,” the company expects to see FY26 growth consistent with its long-term algorithm of 2+% identical sales and adjusted EBITDA growth higher than identical sales growth.

For 2025, however, the company is projecting an adjusted profit of $2.03 to $2.16 per share, falling below the consensus estimate of $2.30 per share. Additionally, identical sales are expected to increase 1.5% to 2.5% and adjusted EBITDA of $3.8B to $3.9B versus 2.0% growth in identical sales and adjusted EBITDA of $4.005Bin the previous year.

For the most recently reported quarter, Albertsons’ (NYSE:ACI) revenues were up 2.5% to $18.8B, in-line with Wall Street’s expectations, driven by a 2.3% increase in identical sales. This contributed to an adjusted profit of $0.46 per share, 3 cents above expectations. Gross margin decreased by 60 basis points to 27.4%, largely due to increased sales in its lower margin pharmacy space, as well as an increase in delivery and handling costs tied to a 24% increase in online sales. Additionally, Albertsons (NYSE:ACI) increased its loyalty members by 15% to 45.6M.

During FY24, capital expenditures were $1.93M, which primarily included the completion of 127 remodels, the opening of 11 new stores and continued investment in our digital and technology platforms.

Finally, the board approved an unchanged quarterly dividend of $0.15 per share, payable on May 9 to shareholders of record as of April 25.

data.title: CEO of Wellgistics Health converts $1.5M of debt to equity at IPO price
data.description:
• Wellgistics Health (NASDAQ:WGRX) has entered into an amendment to the membership interest purchase agreement with Wellgistics and its former owners to convert $1.5M debt into 333,333 shares of the company's common stock at the initial public offering price of $4.50 per share.
  • The amount was controlled by CEO Brian Norton and would have been needed to pay by June 14, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Primis Financial receives stay extension on Nasdaq's delisting determination
data.description:
Bank holding company Primis Financial (NASDAQ:FRST) announced on Monday the receipt of a stay extension on Nasdaq's delisting determination for the company's securities.

The company had received a notice from Nasdaq on April 3 about non-compliance with the stock exchange's listing requirements. Primis had failed to timely file its Form 10-K for the period ending December 31, 2024.

On April 9, the company requested a hearing before the panel and an extension of Nasdaq's stay of delisting.

On April 10, the company received a response from Nasdaq setting the hearing for Thursday, May 15, and staying the delisting determination, pending a final written decision by the Panel.

Primis Financial said it plans to file the Form 10-K for the year ending December 31, 2024, as promptly as practicable, expecting to do so prior to the hearing.

data.title: Apollo critiques 60/40 portfolio as returns fall short since 2022
data.description:
Apollo Asset Management raised concerns over the effectiveness of the traditional 60/40 investment strategy, a long-standing classic approach where investors allocate 60% of their capital to equities and 40% to fixed income. According to the asset manager, this balanced allocation has yielded underwhelming results in recent years.

Despite being hailed for decades as a dependable strategy that balances risk and return, the 60/40 mix has struggled to deliver meaningful performance amid a turbulent market environment shaped by rising interest rates, persistent inflation, and heightened volatility. The conventional belief that bonds would serve as a cushion during equity downturns has been tested, as both asset classes have occasionally moved in tandem, limiting diversification benefits. 

Torsten Sløk, chief economist at Apollo, offered a stark assessment of the portfolio’s recent performance: “The 60/40 portfolio has basically gone nowhere since the beginning of 2022, with only a 2% annual return for the past three and a half years.” 

This commentary reflects growing industry skepticism about whether the traditional allocation can still deliver the risk-adjusted returns investors have come to expect.

See the chart below provided by Apollo Asset Management:

Outlined below are a grouping of popular market tracking and fixed income focused exchange traded funds that many investors may see in a traditional 60/40 portfolio.

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ). 

Fixed Income Tracking ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (NASDAQ:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), (NYSEARCA:BIL), (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).
